reasoning: The US military has engaged in an intensive air and naval campaign against Houthi forces in Yemen in 2025 (Operation Rough Rider). However, the US has consistently avoided deploying regular ground forces to Yemen due to the political and military risks, publicly stating a reliance on airpower, naval assets, and local proxies. The resolution criteria explicitly exclude special operations forces (who might theoretically conduct raids or site exploitations), diplomats, and advisors. There are no credible reports between March and November 2025 of regular US military personnel entering the terrestrial territory of Yemen. With less than two months remaining in 2025, any sudden deployment of conventional ground troops is highly improbable without prior escalation or announcement. Therefore, the likelihood of this occurring and being credibly reported by the end of 2025 is extremely low.
